channel.title: Llm on DAXPY
channel.description: Recent content in Llm on DAXPY

item.title: LoRA
item.description:
LoRA: Low-rank adaptation of large language models

Fine tuning a model can be thought of as computing a weight $\Delta W$ corresponding to each weight matrix $W$ in the model such that the finetuned model’s weight is $W + \Delta W$. LoRA explicitly models this as $W + \Delta W$ and only tunes $\Delta W$ during finetuning, keeping $W$ frozen.
